sanskrit:
इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम्।
इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम्॥
असौ मया हतः शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि।
ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी॥
आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया।
यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिताः॥
transliteration: idam adya mayā labdham imaṃ prāpsye manoratham | idam astīdam api me bhaviṣyati punar dhanam || asau mayā hataḥ śatrur haniṣye cāparān api | īśvaro 'ham ahaṃ bhogī siddho 'haṃ balavān sukhī || āḍhyo 'bhijanavān asmi ko 'nyo 'sti sadṛśo mayā | yakṣye dāsyāmi modiṣya ity ajñāna-vimohitāḥ ||
literal_meaning: This today by me gained; this I shall obtain, my desire. This is mine, this also mine, will be again wealth. That by me slain enemy; I shall slay others too. Lord am I, I the enjoyer, perfect am I, mighty, happy. Wealthy, of noble birth am I; who else is like me? I shall sacrifice, I shall give, I shall rejoice—thus by ignorance deluded.
contextual_meaning: A grandiose, self-inflating inner monologue of the narcissistic-demoniac personality who externalizes blame, hoards resources, and mistakes temporal power for existential security.
psychological_state: Malignant narcissism with antisocial traits; fragile self-esteem armored by omnipotent fantasy and zero-sum competition.
trigger_scenario: A CEO who just crushed a competitor gloats on social media, vows to buy another yacht, and promises charity to ‘give back,’ while secretly dreading the next market dip.
keywords: I’ll give just enough to look good
anchor_text: True power is humility, not hoarding or harming others.
